steps.text: Start with $P = \\dfrac{W}{t}$. To isolate $W$, multiply both sides by $t$: $P \cdot t = \\dfrac{W}{t} \cdot t$.
The $t$ cancels on the right: $W = Pt$.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: $W = Pt$ — correct, matches the derived equation. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: $W = \\dfrac{P}{t}$ — divides instead of multiplies by $t$. Eliminated.
  • C: $W = \\dfrac{t}{P}$ — inverts both variables; does not follow from the algebra. Eliminated.
  • D: $W = P + t$ — adds instead of multiplies. Eliminated.
